NKJV.1: A Song of Ascents. Of David. “If it had not been the LORD who was on our side,” Let Israel now say—
NKJV.2: “If it had not been the LORD who was on our side, When men rose up against us,
NKJV.3: Then they would have swallowed us alive, When their wrath was kindled against us;
NKJV.4: Then the waters would have overwhelmed us, The stream would have gone over our soul;
NKJV.5: Then the swollen waters Would have gone over our soul.”
NKJV.6: Blessed be the LORD, Who has not given us as prey to their teeth.
NKJV.7: Our soul has escaped as a bird from the snare of the fowlers; The snare is broken, and we have escaped.
NKJV.8: Our help is in the name of the LORD, Who made heaven and earth.

ESV.1: If it had not been the LORD who was on our side — let Israel now say —
ESV.2: if it had not been the LORD who was on our side when people rose up against us,
ESV.3: then they would have swallowed us up alive, when their anger was kindled against us;
ESV.4: then the flood would have swept us away, the torrent would have gone over us;
ESV.5: then over us would have gone the raging waters.
ESV.6: Blessed be the LORD, who has not given us as prey to their teeth!
ESV.7: We have escaped like a bird from the snare of the fowlers; the snare is broken, and we have escaped!
ESV.8: Our help is in the name of the LORD, who made heaven and earth.

NASB.1: A Song of Ascents, of David. 'Had it not been the LORD who was on our side,' Let Israel now say,
NASB.2: 'Had it not been the LORD who was on our side When men rose up against us,
NASB.3: Then they would have swallowed us alive, When their anger was kindled against us;
NASB.4: Then the waters would have engulfed us, The stream would have swept over our soul;
NASB.5: Then the raging waters would have swept over our soul.'
NASB.6: Blessed be the LORD, Who has not given us to be torn by their teeth.
NASB.7: Our soul has escaped as a bird out of the snare of the trapper; The snare is broken and we have escaped.
NASB.8: Our help is in the name of the LORD, Who made heaven and earth.

NLT.1:
What if the LORD had not been on our side?
Let all Israel repeat:
NLT.2:
What if the LORD had not been on our side
when people attacked us?
NLT.3:
They would have swallowed us alive
in their burning anger.
NLT.4:
The waters would have engulfed us;
a torrent would have overwhelmed us.
NLT.5:
Yes, the raging waters of their fury
would have overwhelmed our very lives.
NLT.6:
Praise the LORD,
who did not let their teeth tear us apart!
NLT.7:
We escaped like a bird from a hunter’s trap.
The trap is broken, and we are free!
NLT.8:
Our help is from the LORD,
who made heaven and earth.

WEB.1: A Song of Ascents. By David. If it had not been Yahweh who was on our side, let Israel now say,
WEB.2: if it had not been Yahweh who was on our side, when men rose up against us;
WEB.3: then they would have swallowed us up alive, when their wrath was kindled against us;
WEB.4: then the waters would have overwhelmed us, the stream would have gone over our soul;
WEB.5: then the proud waters would have gone over our soul.
WEB.6: Blessed be Yahweh, who has not given us as a prey to their teeth.
WEB.7: Our soul has escaped like a bird out of the fowler’s snare. The snare is broken, and we have escaped.
WEB.8: Our help is in Yahweh’s name, who made heaven and earth.

CSB17.1:
A song of ascents. Of David. [1] If the LORD had not been on our side —
let Israel say —
CSB17.2:
If the LORD had not been on our side ⓛ
when people attacked us, ⓜ
CSB17.3:
then they would have swallowed us alive
in their burning anger against us. ⓝ
CSB17.4:
Then the water would have engulfed us;
the torrent would have swept over us;
CSB17.5: the raging water would have swept over us. ⓐ
CSB17.6:
Blessed be the LORD,
who has not let us be ripped apart by their teeth. ⓑ
CSB17.7:
We have escaped like a bird from the hunter’s net;
the net is torn, and we have escaped. ⓒ
CSB17.8:
Our help is in the name of the LORD,
the Maker of heaven and earth. ⓓ

AMP.1:
A Song of Ascents. Of David. "If it had not been the LORD who was on our side,"
Let Israel now say,
AMP.2:
"If it had not been the LORD who was on our side
When men rose up against us,
AMP.3:
Then they would have [quickly] swallowed us alive,
When their wrath was kindled against us;
AMP.4:
Then the waters would have engulfed us,
The torrent would have swept over our soul;
AMP.5: Then the raging waters would have swept over our soul."
AMP.6:
Blessed be the LORD,
Who has not given us as prey to be torn by their teeth.
AMP.7:
We have escaped like a bird from the snare of the fowlers;
The trap is broken and we have escaped.
AMP.8:
Our help is in the name of the LORD,
Who made heaven and earth.

MSG.1:
A pilgrim song of David
If God hadn't been for us
—all together now, Israel, sing out!—
MSG.2:
If God hadn't been for us
when everyone went against us,
MSG.3:
We would have been swallowed alive
by their violent anger,
MSG.4:
Swept away by the flood of rage,
drowned in the torrent;
MSG.5:
We would have lost our lives
in the wild, raging water.
MSG.6:
Oh, blessed be God!
He didn't go off and leave us.
He didn't abandon us defenseless,
helpless as a rabbit in a pack of snarling dogs.
MSG.7:
We've flown free from their fangs,
free of their traps, free as a bird.
Their grip is broken;
we're free as a bird in flight.
MSG.8:
God's strong name is our help,
the same God who made heaven and earth.

NIV.1:
Psalm 124
A song of ascents. Of David.
If the Lord had not been on our side—
let Israel say—
NIV.2:
if the Lord had not been on our side
when men attacked us,
NIV.3:
when their anger flared against us,
they would have swallowed us alive;
NIV.4:
the flood would have engulfed us,
the torrent would have swept over us,
NIV.5:
the raging waters
would have swept us away.
NIV.6:
Praise be to the Lord,
who has not let us be torn by their teeth.
NIV.7:
We have escaped like a bird
out of the fowler’s snare;
the snare has been broken,
and we have escaped.
NIV.8:
Our help is in the name of the Lord,
the Maker of heaven and earth.

NRSVCE.1:
If it had not been the LORD who was on our side
— let Israel now say —
NRSVCE.2:
if it had not been the LORD who was on our side,
when our enemies attacked us,
NRSVCE.3:
then they would have swallowed us up alive,
when their anger was kindled against us;
NRSVCE.4:
then the flood would have swept us away,
the torrent would have gone over us;
NRSVCE.5:
then over us would have gone
the raging waters.
NRSVCE.6:
Blessed be the LORD,
who has not given us
as prey to their teeth.
NRSVCE.7:
We have escaped like a bird
from the snare of the fowlers;
the snare is broken,
and we have escaped.
NRSVCE.8:
Our help is in the name of the LORD,
who made heaven and earth.

NET.1: “If the LORD had not been on our side” — let Israel say this! —
NET.2: if the LORD had not been on our side, when men attacked us,
NET.3: they would have swallowed us alive, when their anger raged against us.
NET.4: The water would have overpowered us; the current would have overwhelmed us.
NET.5: The raging water would have overwhelmed us.
NET.6: The LORD deserves praise, for he did not hand us over as prey to their teeth.
NET.7: We escaped with our lives, like a bird from a hunter’s snare. The snare broke, and we escaped.
NET.8: Our deliverer is the LORD, the Creator of heaven and earth.
